title: snappy fatty uruguayan monsoon grill
description: a hearty, fatty, and snappy dish originating from uruguay, made with a mixture of bread and meat, perfect for a rainy day or a monsoon

ingredients.name: uruguayan chimichurri
ingredients.notes: see recipe notes for chimichurri ingredients

instructions: in a shallow dish, mix together flour, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of pepper.
in another shallow dish, beat the eggs.
in a third shallow dish, mix together panko breadcrumbs and a pinch of salt.
dredge the beef short ribs in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
dip the floured short ribs in the eggs, making sure they're fully coated.
roll the egg-coated short ribs in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing the crumbs onto the meat to help them stick.
place the breaded short ribs on the grill and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
transfer the cooked short ribs to a plate and let them rest for 5 minutes.
meanwhile, mix the uruguayan chimichurri sauce with the grated cheddar cheese (if using).
to assemble the dish, place a cooked short rib on a plate, spoon some of the chimichurri-cheese mixture on top, and garnish with chopped parsley.
